title: CircularPatternFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing circular pattern features in a component and supports the ability to create new circular pattern features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new circular pattern feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a CircularPatternFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the circular pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CircularPatternFeatureInput object.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified circular pattern feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified circular pattern feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of circular pattern features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: CircularPattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new circular pattern feature.

add.title: CircularPatternFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new circular pattern feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created CircularPatternFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A CircularPatternFeatureInput object that defines the desired circular pattern. Use the createInput method to create a new CircularPatternFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the CircularPatternFeatureInput object) to define the circular pattern.

related_samples.name: circularPatternFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the circularPatternFeatures.add method. To use the sample have a design open that contains at least one body. When you run the script, it will prompt you to select a body, which will be patterned around the base Y-axis.

related_samples.name: CircularPattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new circular pattern feature.

classType.title: CircularPatternFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: CircularPatternFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a CircularPatternFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the circular pattern you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the CircularPatternFeatureInput object.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created CircularPatternFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: inputEntities
parameters.description: The collection can contain faces, features, bodies or occurrences. All of the entities must be of a single type. For example, it can't contain features and occurrences but only features or occurrences.

parameters.name: axis
parameters.description: Input linear entity or the entity has axis that defines axis of circular pattern. This can be a sketch line, linear edge, construction axis, an edge/sketch curve that defines an axis (circle, etc.) or a face that defines an axis (cylinder, cone, torus, etc.).

related_samples.name: circularPatternFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the circularPatternFeatures.add method. To use the sample have a design open that contains at least one body. When you run the script, it will prompt you to select a body, which will be patterned around the base Y-axis.

related_samples.name: CircularPattern Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new circular pattern feature.

item.title: CircularPatternFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified circular pattern feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: CircularPatternFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified circular pattern feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: CircularPatternFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of circular pattern features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circularPatternFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: CircularPatternFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circularPatternFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: CircularPatternFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = circularPatternFeatures_var.objectType
